pr.title: feat: Add automated social media posting system
pr.body:
## Summary

Adds a complete automated social media posting system that can publish content to **Twitter/X**, **Facebook**, and **Instagram**.

**Key components:**

- **Python bot** (`social_media_bot/`) — modular platform adapters for Twitter (Tweepy v2), Facebook (Graph API), and Instagram (Graph API), with a CLI entry point supporting `--dry-run`, `--post-id`, and `--platforms` flags
- **GitHub Actions workflows:**
  - `social-media-post.yml` — daily scheduled posting (09:00 UTC cron) + manual `workflow_dispatch` with configurable platforms, post selection, and dry-run mode
  - `validate-config.yml` — CI validation that runs on every push/PR to verify YAML config integrity and dry-run post selection
- **YAML content config** (`content/posts.yaml`) — declarative post definitions with text, media attachments (images, GIFs), scheduled dates, and per-post platform targeting
- **Credential security** — all API tokens managed exclusively via GitHub Secrets (never stored in code)
- **Documentation** (`SOCIAL_MEDIA_AUTOMATION.md`) — complete setup guide covering API credential configuration, content authoring, trigger modes, local testing, and extensibility

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] **Add API credentials as GitHub Secrets** — the bot will not post without these. Follow the table in `SOCIAL_MEDIA_AUTOMATION.md` for the required secret names for each platform (Twitter: 5 secrets, Facebook: 2, Instagram: 2)
- [ ] **Test with a dry run first** — go to Actions → Social Media Auto-Post → Run workflow, set `dry_run` to `true`, and verify the output selects the correct post
- [ ] **Test a real post** — after adding secrets, run the workflow with `dry_run` set to `false` against a single platform (e.g., just `twitter`) to verify end-to-end posting works
- [ ] **Verify the cron schedule** — the daily trigger is set to `0 9 * * *` (09:00 UTC). Adjust if a different time or frequency is needed
- [ ] **Review post content** in `content/posts.yaml` — the sample posts reference the site's services. Edit or add posts as needed

### Notes

- Instagram requires images hosted at publicly accessible URLs (not local files). Use the `instagram_image_url` field in `posts.yaml` for this.
- To add a new platform, create a module in `social_media_bot/platforms/`, implement a `post(text, media_paths)` method, and register it in `poster.py`'s `PLATFORM_MAP`.
- The `validate-config.yml` CI workflow runs automatically on PRs touching `content/`, `social_media_bot/`, or `requirements.txt`.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/b0cc7810052943149d29122bbb7fb638
Requested by: @randomused786-gif
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
